metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2230
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.1: "Spine changes induced by Stau1 siRNA are prevented by NMDA receptor blockade and high Mg2+"
Fig.2: "Impairment of FSK-induced L-LTP after Stau1 knockdown during high Mg2+ treatment"

Together: mRNA binding/transporting protein Stau1 regulates spine morphology and late-LTP induction. The role in late-LTP induction is independent of the role in morphological changes. Literal: "In conclusion, we found that Stau1 involvement in spine morphogenesis is dependent on NMDA receptor-mediated plasticity in hippocampal pyramidal cells. We also found that Stau1 is required for late-LTP, independently of its role in spine morphogenesis"

Note: the role as mRNA binding/transporting protein is not shown in this paper.. Experimental description: - Slice culture: The species of the cells is not stated in the paper. I have derived the species, rat, from a remark on the specificity of the shRNA sequences: "siRNA target sequences for rat were as described"
